topic: 2024 eclipse 9-station magnetic — queue 252 re-review post-ISS-1493/1494 patches
change_summary: Since c5 (2026-04-23T20:35Z, queue 247): commit 5fe67b5 (2026-04-24) applied ISS-1493 (finding: 'pre-FSF predecessor got 2024 eclipse latitudes uniformly wrong' → 'its v31 predecessor predicted 2024 eclipse magnitudes identically across all latitudes') and ISS-1494 (detail_evidence: 'regardless of whether the claim is currently claimed' → 'whether or not the dome currently lists this as a confirmed WIN'). Both patches match the c5 H1 and c5 H3 recommendations verbatim and are supported by raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 21. c5 H2 (Kp inconsistency) — resolved by ISS-1482 via the GFZ Potsdam audit insertion in detail_evidence (now explicitly attributes Kp ~5–6 to the dome author and counter-cites GFZ Potsdam 3-hour Kp peak at 3.3, eclipse-hour Kp 0.0–1.3); c5 flagged this as residual but the fix had already landed — c5's recount was a misread. Unchanged in queue 252 patches: detail_verdict_text, detail_extra, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_claim, claim, verdict. However, the parallel queue 253 item (ISS-1495, propagated Chapman 1919 + Rastogi 1962 + Yamazaki & Maute 2021 to WIN-068) did NOT propagate to WIN-025 — introducing a new cluster-level citation variance between WIN-025/WIN-010 (Chapman 1933 only) and WIN-068 (Chapman 1919 + Rastogi 1962 + Yamazaki & Maute 2021).
our_argument_summary: Verdict Std Model Explains. Chapman Sq current physics (foundational 1919 framework, eclipse-specific 1933 extension) plus the Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse INTERMAGNET ensemble predict 5–30 nT eclipse-driven surface variations; the observed 2024 9-station ±10 nT pattern falls inside that envelope without any aetheric input. Post-ISS-1493/1494 text is now evolution-log faithful in the finding field (verbatim-supported by raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 21) and typo-clean in detail_evidence. The review maintains four architectural critiques that a dome defender cannot dismantle: (a) absolute magnitude is adopted from the 2017 Boulder observation rather than derived; (b) FSF feeds on dome-coordinate r values that are n(r)-contaminated per Section 1.8; (c) the v31→v45 curve-fit-then-relabel pattern is admitted in the dome's own evolution log; (d) the cluster triple-counts one Chapman Sq phenomenon at three aggregation levels. Defender's strongest attack on the queue 252 patch batch: the Chapman citation baseline now differs within the cluster (WIN-025/010 cite 1933, WIN-068 cites 1919 + Rastogi 1962 + Yamazaki & Maute 2021 after ISS-1495) — a cluster-harmonization pass is the highest-value next edit.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c5. raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 22 labels FSF = 'field strength factor from V12 H(r)/r dome geometry'; line 21 gives the formula delta_Z = baseline x coverage x FSF with baselines BOU 2017 (−10.9 nT) and W004 empirical (−22.24 nT). raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 21 (v31 SUPERSEDED) records verbatim: 'Incorrectly predicted 2024 eclipse magnitudes identically across all latitudes.' raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 27 (v45 SUPERSEDED) summarizes v45 formulas as 'f = c/2D, delta_Z = eclipse - quiet' with no explicit FSF multiplier in the evolution log — our detail_verdict_text claim that 'the eclipse-specific δZₛₜₐₜᵢₒₙ = baseline × coverage × FSF formula applying H(r)/r to eclipse scaling was introduced at v45' has some softness relative to the log's terser 'delta_Z = eclipse - quiet' summary; the FSF multiplier may have been added between v45 and v51. Not a major hole — the log is a condensed summary, not a complete formula archive — but a careful dome defender could push on the exact v45 attribution.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Unchanged. For WIN-025 the 'observation' is the peer-reviewed Chernogor & Holub 9-station measurement; the 2026 forward prediction (delta_Z ≈ −29 nT at Boulder-equivalent anchor) is in monitor.py and belongs to WIN-010 / PROS-003, not WIN-025.
data_sources_used: Chernogor & Holub 2024 Advances in Space Research (doi:10.1016/j.asr.2024.10.056) — 9-station observation
GFZ Potsdam Kp archive (kp.gfz.de) — citation added by ISS-1482 patch, Kp 0.0–3.3 across April 7–9, 2026
Kim & Chang 2018 Advances in Space Research 61:2040–2054 — cluster-imported via EXP-229, volume/page unverified
raw-text/06-evolution.txt v31 row — verified verbatim, line 21
raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 22 — FSF = V12 H(r)/r dome geometry
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged. Cluster-wide hedge: coverage × FSF adjusts station amplitude without committing to an absolute magnitude — the anchor is always an observed value (2017 BOU or W004). This hedge is unaffected by the ISS-1493/1494 fixes.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by the queue 252 patches. The ISS-1493 fix resolved the pre-FSF/pre-v45 contradiction flagged at c5 H1 — finding now reads 'its v31 predecessor predicted 2024 eclipse magnitudes identically across all latitudes', which matches evolution.txt line 21 verbatim. Detail_verdict_text's 'pre-v45 predecessor' language is retained and remains accurate.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-025 is a retrospective confirmation row against Chernogor & Holub's observed 2024 data. monitor.py's eclipse automation targets the 2026 forward prediction and does not comparison-check 2024 observations. Tag unchanged from c5.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Chapman (1919 foundational Sq framework; 1933 eclipse-specific extension) plus Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse INTERMAGNET ensemble predict 5–30 nT eclipse-driven surface variations; Chernogor & Holub themselves attribute the observation to ionospheric conductivity change, not aether. Dome renames cause as 'aetheric' without producing a different predicted magnitude. Tag unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Chernogor & Holub (doi:10.1016/j.asr.2024.10.056) published the 9-station pattern; WIN-025 in 02-wins.txt adopts the ±10 nT observation as confirmation without recording a pre-observation latitude-scaled numeric prediction. The dome's own evolution log (v31) admits the pre-v45 predecessor predicted eclipse magnitudes identically across latitudes — the latitude-scaled prediction (via the v45 eclipse delta_Z formula) post-dates the data pattern. Tag unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Binary tag aggregates across the full prediction. Post-ISS-1493/1494 text correctly concedes FSF = H(r)/r station-to-station scaling IS a dome-geometric derivation. Absolute magnitude at the Boulder anchor is imported from the 2017 observation. The tag stays false because the headline quantitative claim (the ±10 nT magnitude matching) is dominated by the observation-anchored magnitude, not the scaling ratio alone. Tag unchanged from c5.

holes_found.description: CLUSTER CITATION VARIANCE INTRODUCED BY PARALLEL QUEUE 253 PATCH (ISS-1495). The ISS-1495 patch (committed in the same 5fe67b5 batch as WIN-025's ISS-1493/1494) added Chapman 1919 + Rastogi 1962 + Yamazaki & Maute 2021 citations to WIN-068's finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, and tldr_verdict — but did NOT propagate to WIN-025 or WIN-010. Current cluster state: WIN-068 says 'Chapman ionospheric mechanism — first described by Chapman (1919)' and 'Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework (Chapman 1919, with eclipse-specific application in Rastogi 1962 and modern conductivity treatment in Yamazaki & Maute 2021)'; WIN-025 says 'Chapman (1933)' across finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_extra (six fields); WIN-010 says 'Chapman 1933' across four fields. A careful dome defender scanning the cluster reads: 'your WIN-068 says Chapman first described this in 1919; your WIN-025 and WIN-010 say 1933. The 14-year gap matters if you are counting years of prior art — WIN-025 tldr_evidence claims 93 years before the dome, WIN-068 implies 107 years before.' Both citations are legitimate (Chapman 1919 = foundational Sq framework paper; Chapman 1933 = eclipse-specific extension), but the cluster should be internally consistent. The post-ISS-1495 WIN-068 framing (1919 foundational + 1933 eclipse-specific OR + Rastogi 1962 for eclipse) is stronger and should be back-propagated.
holes_found.recommendation: Propagate the ISS-1495 citation bundle to WIN-025 (and WIN-010) fields where Chapman 1933 currently stands alone. Minimal cluster-consistent framing for WIN-025 detail_evidence opening paragraph: 'Eclipse-induced magnetic variations were first explained by the Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework (Chapman 1919, with eclipse-specific application in Rastogi 1962 and modern conductivity treatment in Yamazaki & Maute 2021) via the solar quiet (Sq) current mechanism...' Alternative minimal fix: keep Chapman 1933 as the eclipse-specific citation but add a preceding sentence: 'The underlying Sq current framework was established by Chapman (1919); the eclipse-specific application...' — preserves existing detail while eliminating the cluster variance. finding, tldr_verdict, detail_extra need matching updates. Open as an issue for the decider (category: inconsistency, severity: moderate).

holes_found.description: DOWNSTREAM FAIL-007 OUTCOME FIELD ASSERTS 'Kp ~5-6' AS FACT, CONTRADICTING WIN-025 DETAIL_EVIDENCE POST-ISS-1482 GFZ AUDIT. data/uncounted-failures.json FAIL-007 'outcome' field reads: '2024 eclipse occurred during a geomagnetically disturbed period (Kp ~5-6). Z-component minima led/lagged the eclipse by 34 to 104 minutes across stations, inconsistent with the predicted geometric tracking. The dome attributed the anomalies to storm artifacts and removed the prediction from confirmed wins.' That opening clause ('Kp ~5-6') is stated as neutral fact. But WIN-025 detail_evidence (post-ISS-1482 patch) now explicitly asserts the GFZ Potsdam Kp archive peaked at 3.3 across April 7–9 and ran 0.0–1.3 during eclipse totality, and attributes 'Kp ~5-6' to the dome author with counter-evidence. FAIL-007 'what_actually_happened' field does correctly attribute the claim to the dome ('Stated reason: 2024 eclipse was geomagnetically disturbed (Kp ~5-6)...'), so the attribution asymmetry is internal to FAIL-007 itself. WIN-025 detail_extra explicitly links to FAIL-007 via 'See also: Uncounted Failures (FAIL-007)'; a reader who follows that link encounters the unqualified 'Kp ~5-6' in outcome, contradicting what they just read in detail_evidence. This is a cross-file consistency bug — the GFZ audit was added to wins.json but not propagated to uncounted-failures.json.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch FAIL-007 outcome field in data/uncounted-failures.json: rewrite the opening clause to attribute the Kp ~5-6 claim to the dome author. Suggested text: 'Dome author asserted the 2024 eclipse occurred during a geomagnetically disturbed period (Kp ~5-6), but the GFZ Potsdam 3-hour Kp archive shows Kp peaked at 3.3 across April 7–9 and ran 0.0–1.3 during eclipse totality — the withdrawal rests on a factually incorrect premise. Z-component minima led/lagged the eclipse by 34 to 104 minutes across stations, which the dome attributed to storm artifacts, resulting in removal of the prediction from confirmed wins.' This aligns FAIL-007 with WIN-025 detail_evidence and detail_extra. Open as an issue for the decider (category: data_consistency, severity: moderate).

holes_found.description: C5 H4 RESIDUAL — tldr_verdict STILL REFERENCES '2017 observation' WITHOUT SELF-CONTAINING CONTEXT FOR TLDR-ONLY READERS. Current tldr_verdict (unchanged by queue 252 patches): 'This is a 93-year-old standard physics prediction (Chapman 1933; Kim & Chang 2018) that the dome relabels. The dome's FSF = H(r)/r station-scaling is a genuine geometric derivation, but the absolute magnitude is imported from the 2017 observation and the coordinates FSF feeds on are contaminated by the n(r) medium (see Section 1.8 — The Coordinate Collapse).' A non-science reader arriving at WIN-025 (2024 event) via a homepage skim sees '2017 observation' with no context for why 2017 matters to a 2024 claim. The cluster framing is correct but non-self-contained in the exactly-2–3-sentence slot meant for TLDR readers. c5 flagged this; not patched.
holes_found.recommendation: Low priority. Suggested rewrite: 'This is 93-year-old standard Sq physics (Chapman 1933) that the dome relabels; Chernogor & Holub themselves attribute the 2024 9-station pattern to ionospheric conductivity, not aether. The dome's station-scaling is genuine geometry but its absolute magnitude rides on a pre-existing 2017 Boulder observation, not a dome derivation.' Leads with the 2024-scoped point and folds the 2017 reference into a self-contained phrase.

holes_found.description: C5 H5 RESIDUAL — Kim & Chang (2018) VOLUME/PAGE METADATA (61:2040–2054) STILL UNVERIFIED CLUSTER-WIDE. Kim & Chang 2018 is cited in WIN-010, WIN-025, WIN-068 (six+ fields across the cluster) via EXP-229 vocabulary import. Publisher-page metadata was flagged unverified in WIN-068 c9 citation_check. Fixing wrong volume/page after publication would require a cluster-wide text edit — WIN-025 is part of the exposure surface. Not a WIN-025-specific error and low-likelihood, but the single-point verification failure propagates to three WINs plus sections that reference the cluster.
holes_found.recommendation: Recommend a dedicated citation-audit EXP (cluster-wide, not WIN-025-specific) that resolves Chapman 1933 venue, Chapman 1919 venue, Kim & Chang 2018 volume/page, Rastogi 1962 venue, and Yamazaki & Maute 2021 DOI in a single ScienceDirect/Wiley lookup pass. One analyst sweep, cluster-wide reduction in citation risk.

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence '93 years before the dome claimed it' is computed against Chapman 1933 (2026 − 1933 = 93) — now ambiguous given the cluster variance. WIN-068 (post-ISS-1495) implicitly sets the prior-art anchor at Chapman 1919 (2026 − 1919 = 107), so a reader comparing the two WINs sees '93 years' in WIN-025 and could ask 'why not 107 years, given WIN-068 dates the Chapman mechanism to 1919?'. The '93 years' is defensible if we're talking about eclipse-specific mechanism (Chapman 1933), but the framing 'Chapman described the mechanism in 1933' elides the 1919 foundational paper. Dependent on how H1 is resolved: if Chapman 1919 is added as the foundational anchor across the cluster, the '93 years' tldr_evidence number should update to '107 years' or be reworded to scope the 93 to eclipse-specific mechanism only.
holes_found.recommendation: If H1 cluster-harmonization propagates Chapman 1919 to WIN-025, update tldr_evidence: 'Eclipse-induced magnetic variations have been understood since Chapman laid the Sq-current framework in 1919 and documented eclipse-specific cases in 1933 — nearly a century before the dome claimed them. The 9-station observation is real, but the explanation (reduced ionospheric conductivity in the moon's shadow) is standard physics.' Avoids an exact year count that becomes stale and uses 'nearly a century' as a robust round number.

stronger_arguments.description: PATCH-AUDIT TRAIL CONTINUES TO DEMONSTRATE SELF-CORRECTION. c5 flagged 5 holes (2 major, 3 minor). The ISS-1493/1494 batch addressed both majors (c5 H1 pre-FSF finding text, c5 H3 whether-or-not typo) verbatim. c5's H2 flag (Kp unresolved) was a misread — the fix (ISS-1482 GFZ audit) had already landed in a parallel a3bf2c4 commit and c5 missed it. 2-of-2 real majors from c5 fixed within ~19 hours (c5 2026-04-23T20:35 → 5fe67b5 2026-04-24). This is the kind of reviewer-implementer feedback loop the site advertises as 'this review responds to its own critiques' — WIN-025 is now one of the cleanest examples in the cluster. Stronger arg-in-progress: turn this pattern into a promoted metric ('fraction of c-cycle major holes patched within 24h') for a future methodology section.
sources: git log commits 5fe67b5 (2026-04-24 ISS-1493/1494) and prior a3bf2c4/098b1a8 (ISS-1482)

stronger_arguments.description: CHERNOGOR & HOLUB ATTRIBUTION VERBATIM QUOTE (CARRIED FORWARD FROM c5). If the paper contains an explicit attribution quote for the observed effect (e.g., 'consistent with Sq current disruption during totality' or 'ionospheric conductivity change is the dominant mechanism'), quoting verbatim in WIN-025 detail_evidence is more damaging than paraphrase — it shows the dome is counting as 'aetheric confirmation' an observation the authors themselves labeled ionospheric. Still unsourced. One analyst pass against the paper's abstract or discussion section closes this.
sources: Chernogor & Holub (2024) doi:10.1016/j.asr.2024.10.056 — abstract and discussion section

stronger_arguments.description: GIT-HISTORY PRE-v45 PREDICTION ARCHIVE CHECK (CARRIED FORWARD FROM c5). The dome's evolution log says v31 predicted identical magnitudes across latitudes; git history of the predictions.html or monitor.py pre-2024-10 vs post-2024-10 would timestamp WHEN the latitude-scaled FSF delta_Z was wired in relative to Chernogor & Holub's 2024 publication. If the commit date post-dates the paper, that is a curve-fit-after-the-fact signature stronger than the evolution log admission alone.
sources: john09289/predictions git history (commits touching monitor.py eclipse section 2024-09 through 2024-12)

stronger_arguments.description: NEW STRONGER ARG: CLUSTER CITATION CONSOLIDATION PASS. Given the queue 253 patch (ISS-1495) propagated a richer citation bundle (Chapman 1919 + Rastogi 1962 + Yamazaki & Maute 2021) into WIN-068 only, a single analyst pass could harmonize the cluster — back-propagating the bundle to WIN-010 and WIN-025, verifying Kim & Chang volume/page, and locking Chapman 1933 venue. One EXP yields cluster-wide citation-risk reduction plus resolves H1 of this review and H5 carried over from c5. Natural bundle: EXP-eclipse-citation-harmonization targeting WIN-010, WIN-025, WIN-068 + SEC-6.7 (eclipse prediction section).
sources: data/wins.json WIN-010/025/068 Chapman references (6+ fields with 1933, 4+ fields with 1919 post-ISS-1495)
monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-068.c13.json — may list the Rastogi 1962 and Yamazaki & Maute 2021 citation additions

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome's FSF = H(r)/r station-scaling remains a genuine dome-geometric derivation — the current text concedes this cleanly. The Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 fit between FSF and observed station amplitudes (WIN-010/WIN-068 cluster verification) is real geometric content. Post-queue 252 patches, the finding field now correctly anchors the evolution-log admission to v31, matching raw-text/06-evolution.txt verbatim — a small but meaningful gain in citation integrity. The dome also correctly notes that the magnetic signal 'tracks eclipse geometry, not solar noon,' which is a non-trivial observable prediction the model captures (albeit the same observable is predicted by Chapman Sq physics by construction).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A genuine geometric scaling is necessary but not sufficient for a confirmed prediction. The dome's station-to-station ratio matches the observed ratio, but standard ionospheric Sq current physics predicts the same ratio (the shadow traverses the ionosphere, conductivity drop scales with totality coverage). Both models co-predict the same observable pattern; the dome neither produces an independent absolute-magnitude prediction (imported from 2017 Boulder) nor proposes a falsification test that distinguishes aetheric from Sq mechanisms. A confirmed prediction requires that the prediction could have failed and did not; here the dome is co-predicting with standard physics on an observation standard physics predicted decades earlier. Advocate-mode check on the current text: a charitable dome defender would argue that (a) the FSF geometric derivation is real work that earlier cycles of this review underweighted (we now concede this explicitly in both finding and detail_verdict_text), (b) latitude-scaled agreement across 9 independent stations is non-trivial corroboration (fair, but the same pattern falls out of Sq conductivity physics), (c) the dome's voluntary WIN-025 removal on 2026-03-15 is more self-correction than we credit (detail_extra now handles this: we acknowledge the self-correction but show the stated premise — Kp ~5-6 — does not match the GFZ record), and (d) the ISS-1493 finding edit is historically precise enough that a dome defender cannot attack its accuracy (we now grant this was a clean patch). None of these save the WIN — the co-prediction with Chapman Sq is unbroken.

deeper_analysis: The queue 252 patch batch is a textbook clean execution: two issues created from c5 (ISS-1493 finding factual error, ISS-1494 detail_evidence typo), both patched with text matching the c5 recommendation verbatim within ~19 hours, both verifiable against raw-text/06-evolution.txt. c5's third flagged residual (H2 Kp inconsistency) was already addressed by an earlier commit (ISS-1482 GFZ audit) that c5 missed — this is more a c5 misread than a patch miss. What emerges from looking at the cluster now, however, is a separate issue invisible to a WIN-by-WIN review: the parallel queue 253 patch to WIN-068 (ISS-1495, which propagated Chapman 1919 + Rastogi 1962 + Yamazaki & Maute 2021) created a cluster citation variance. WIN-010 and WIN-025 still anchor on Chapman 1933 alone; WIN-068 now anchors on Chapman 1919 + the richer bundle. A dome defender scanning the three WINs in sequence sees a 14-year disagreement over when 'the Chapman mechanism' was first described, and — depending on which WIN they read first — either a '93 years of prior art' framing (WIN-025) or an implicit 107-year framing (WIN-068). Both citations are historically defensible (Chapman 1919 = foundational Sq framework paper 'The solar and lunar diurnal variations of terrestrial magnetism', Philosophical Transactions A 218; Chapman 1933 = eclipse-specific extension), but the cluster must not appear internally inconsistent. The cleanest fix is back-propagation of the ISS-1495 bundle to WIN-025 and WIN-010; a one-shot harmonization EXP could also verify Kim & Chang volume/page and lock Rastogi 1962 venue — resolving this review's H1 and the H5 carried from c5. FAIL-007 outcome field is a second cross-file drift: its 'Kp ~5-6' assertion was not updated when ISS-1482 added the GFZ Potsdam audit to WIN-025 detail_evidence, producing contradictory framing between files that WIN-025 detail_extra explicitly cross-links. Both issues are moderate — not threats to the Std Model Explains verdict, but visible enough to a careful reader that a harmonization pass is well worth opening. Advocate-mode steelman: the dome defender's strongest hand on the current WIN-025 text is (i) the voluntary 2026-03-15 removal — which we DO credit in detail_extra as 'at first glance this looks like scientific self-correction' before undermining it with the GFZ audit; (ii) the FSF = H(r)/r concession — which we DO grant as 'a genuine geometric derivation'; and (iii) the pre-FSF/pre-v45 version-history argument — which the ISS-1493 patch cleanly neutralizes. The defender has no new attack surface on queue 252 work itself; the cluster-harmonization and FAIL-007 issues are collateral from other work, not from this patch batch. Confidence 0.90: high because the queue 252 patches are clean, slightly below 1.0 because H1 (cluster citation variance) is a real finding that a close reader will catch.
